checks.evidence: The developers homepage and GraphQL API docs describe Linear as a modern issue tracking and project planning tool for software teams, consistent with the 'Project Management / Issue Tracking' category.
The GraphQL API docs explicitly state: 'The Linear API supports personal API keys and OAuth2 authentication.' Both methods are confirmed.

The pricing page shows 'API and webhook access' and 'MCP access' both checkmarked on the Free plan ($0). Docs confirm API keys can be self-generated from Settings > Account > Security & Access. No paid plan required for API.

Docs confirm a public GraphQL API at https://api.linear.app/graphql with full CRUD for issues, projects, cycles, teams, users, etc. Webhooks, TypeScript SDK, filtering, pagination, introspection all documented. Rate limit page confirms 2,500 req/h (API key), 5,000 (OAuth), 600 (unauthenticated).

The pricing page shows 'MCP access' as a checkmarked feature on the Free plan (and all paid plans), confirming MCP exists as an offering. However, the specific endpoint URL (https://mcp.linear.app/mcp) and 'official' designation could not be verified from the rendered pages provided.
Public GraphQL API, self-serve API keys on free plan, OAuth2, webhooks, TypeScript SDK, and MCP access are all confirmed. No material blockers observed. The verdict 'buildable_today' is supported.
